channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: WHO Raises Ebola Alert in DR Congo Amid Rising Cases
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has raised the Ebola outbreak risk in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) to the highest level, calling the situation deeply concerning. Currently, there are abo...

item.title: La Réunion and South Africa: Decades of Missed Cooperation
item.description: Over forty years ago, leaders from La Réunion secretly supported Nelson Mandela’s ANC, with Paul Vergès even securing early European sanctions against apartheid South Africa. Despite this shared histo...

item.title: Government to review sentences of three boys convicted of rape following public outcry
item.description: The UK government has announced a review of the sentences handed to three boys, aged 14 and 15, who were convicted of raping two girls in separate incidents in Fordingbridge, Hampshire. The boys recei...

item.title: Rising Beef Prices Impact Summer BBQs in Canada
item.description: As Canadians prepare for summer barbecues, beef prices remain significantly higher than previous years, with costs up 62.6 per cent since 2021. The increase is largely due to several years of drought ...

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard steps down as US intelligence chief due to husband's illness
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard announced her resignation from the post of Director of National Intelligence under President Donald Trump, citing her husband's recent diagnosis with a rare type of bone cancer as the re...

item.title: Beloved TV Presenter Judith Chalmers Passes Away at 90
item.description: Judith Chalmers, the cherished British television presenter renowned for hosting ITV's long-running travel programme Wish You Were Here...?, has passed away at the age of 90. Her family announced that...

item.title: Canada Implements Enhanced Ebola Screening at Airports Following Negative Ontario Case
item.description: Canada has implemented enhanced screening measures at airports for travellers returning from African countries affected by a recent Ebola outbreak. This step follows the proactive testing of an indivi...

item.title: Quinta Brunson to develop and star in Betty Boop’s first major film in decades
item.description: Quinta Brunson, known for her Emmy-winning comedy series Abbott Elementary and her voice role as Dr Fuzzby in Zootopia 2, is set to both develop and star in a new Betty Boop feature film. This upcomin...

item.title: Community Lottery in Manitoba Surpasses $200,000 Jackpot
item.description: In Manitoba, the local Chase the Ace lottery has seen its jackpot grow significantly, exceeding $200,000. This community-driven fundraising initiative has captured the interest and excitement of resid...

item.title: Jim Carrey's Early Life: Childhood and Teenage Years in Ontario
item.description: Born on January 17, 1962, Jim Carrey grew up in Ontario, Canada, in a lower-middle-class family. The youngest of four children, he was the son of Percy, a trained saxophonist turned accountant, and Ka...

item.title: RSS Leaders Reflect on the Organisation's Role During Partition
item.description: Sunil Ambekar, national publicity chief of the RSS, recently spoke at the screening of the documentary “Delhi Mein Sangh Yatra” in Delhi, discussing the organisation's role during the Partition era. H...

item.title: Hiker rescued from ledge on Table Mountain after fall
item.description: A routine morning hike turned into a life-threatening situation for a 50-year-old local man on Table Mountain. When he did not return home, his worried wife used the Life360 app to pinpoint his GPS lo...

item.title: Former attorney accused of stealing R2.2 million from clients granted bail
item.description: A former attorney, 43-year-old Clea Hattingh, appeared in the White River magistrate’s court after allegedly misappropriating more than R2.2 million from three separate clients’ trust and estate funds...

item.title: Private Texts Show Controversial Comments by Senator John Fetterman
item.description: Private messages obtained by New York Magazine reveal a series of controversial comments made by U.S. Senator John Fetterman (D-PA). In the texts, Fetterman reportedly dismissed the significance of th...

item.title: Treasury defends CGT changes that could impact individual share investors
item.description: The Australian Treasury has responded to criticism regarding proposed changes to the capital gains tax (CGT), which are part of Treasurer Jim Chalmers' budget. According to the Australian Financial Re...

item.title: ISKCON to Provide Nutritious Meals to Patients at Government Wenlock Hospital Starting June 1
item.description: From 1st June, ISKCON will begin serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner to patients at the Government Wenlock Hospital in Mangaluru, as announced by the Minister for Health and Family Welfare, Dinesh Gu...

item.title: Trump explains absence from Donald Trump Jr.'s wedding, citing government duties
item.description: President Donald Trump announced on social media that he would not attend the wedding of his son, Donald Trump Jr., and his fiancée, Bettina Anderson, citing responsibilities related to the government...

item.title: David Alaba to depart Real Madrid as contract ends
item.description: David Alaba, the Austrian defender, don confirm say him go leave Real Madrid at the end of this season as him contract don reach expiry. The 33-year-old player don dey with the Spanish side for five y...

item.title: Guy Garvey on Robert Smith, new Elbow album, and support for grassroots music venues
item.description: Guy Garvey, frontman of Elbow, spoke to NME at the 2026 Ivor Novello Awards about his friendship with Robert Smith of The Cure and the band’s plans for a new album. Garvey described Smith as a “wonder...

item.title: WHO warns Congo faces very high national risk of Ebola outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has raised the risk of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola spreading across the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) to a 'very high' level nationally. This strain does no...
